cases.quote: Chlorophyll absorbs light most strongly in the blue and red regions.

chunks.text: The Calvin cycle occurs in the stroma and uses carbon dioxide, ATP, and NADPH.
Chlorophyll absorbs light most strongly in the blue and red regions. Green light is largely reflected.
Stomata permit carbon dioxide entry but can increase water loss.

cases.quote: B-tree indexes are commonly used for equality lookups and range queries.

chunks.text: Transactions group database operations into an atomic unit.
Normalization reduces duplicated data across relational tables.
B-tree indexes are commonly used for equality lookups and range queries.

cases.query: pancreatic alpha cells glucagon low blood glucose
cases.quote: Alpha cells in the pancreas release glucagon when blood glucose falls.

chunks.text: Beta cells release insulin after blood glucose rises following a meal.
Alpha cells in the pancreas release glucagon when blood glucose falls. Glucagon promotes hepatic glucose release.
Type 1 diabetes results from autoimmune destruction of beta cells.

cases.query: ETag validator 304 Not Modified cached representation
cases.quote: The server can return 304 Not Modified instead of the full body.

chunks.text: Cookies store small values associated with a site.
ETag is a validator for a cached representation. The server can return 304 Not Modified instead of the full body.
Personalized responses require careful cache controls.

cases.quote: The spindle checkpoint delays chromosome separation.

chunks.text: The G1 checkpoint checks nutrients and DNA damage.
Checkpoint signaling can pause the cycle so repair can occur.
The spindle checkpoint delays chromosome separation until chromosomes attach correctly.
